import os

SCREENSHOT_DIR = Path("tmp_re14_screenshots")

TOPIC = "基于大语言模型的医学问答可信度评估方法研究"


@pytest.fixture
async def browser_page():
    async with async_playwright() as p:
        browser = await p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        context = await browser.new_context(viewport={"width": 1280, "height": 900}, locale="zh-CN")
        page = await context.new_page()
        errors = []
        page.on("console", lambda msg: errors.append(msg.text) if msg.type == "error" else None)
        page.on("pageerror", lambda err: errors.append(str(err)))
        yield page, errors
        await browser.close()

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""Re1.4 Playwright E2E — 前端全流程截图验证。

运行方式:
    # 先启动 API 服务
    cd apps/api && python -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 18181 &
    
    # 再运行测试
    python -m pytest apps/web/e2e/test_re1_4_frontend.py -s --tb=short --asyncio-mode=auto
"""
import os
import asyncio
from pathlib import Path

import pytest
from playwright.async_api import async_playwright

BASE_URL = "http://127.0.0.1:18181"
SCREENSHOT_DIR = Path("tmp_re14_screenshots")
SCREENSHOT_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
HISTORY_CASE_ID = os.environ.get("RE14_HISTORY_CASE", "re14-medical-llm")
TOPIC = "基于大语言模型的医学问答可信度评估方法研究"


@pytest.fixture
async def browser_page():
    async with async_playwright() as p:
        browser = await p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        context = await browser.new_context(viewport={"width": 1280, "height": 900}, locale="zh-CN")
        page = await context.new_page()
        errors = []
        page.on("console", lambda msg: errors.append(msg.text) if msg.type == "error" else None)
        page.on("pageerror", lambda err: errors.append(str(err)))
        yield page, errors
        await browser.close()


class TestFrontendE2E:
    """17 项 Playwright E2E 测试，每步截图。"""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_01_page_loads(self, browser_page):
        page, errors = browser_page
        await page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/web/")
        await page.wait_for_selector("h1", timeout=10000)
        await page.screenshot(path=str(SCREENSHOT_DIR / "01_page_load.png"))
        assert len(errors) == 0, f"Console errors: {errors}"
        assert "PaperAgent" in await page.title()

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_02_topic_input(self, browser_page):
        page, errors = browser_page
        await page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/web/")
        await page.fill("#topic", TOPIC)
        await page.screenshot(path=str(SCREENSHOT_DIR / "02_topic_input.png"))
        assert "医学问答" in await page.input_value("#topic")

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_03_submit_and_progress(self, browser_page):
        page, errors = browser_page
        await page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/web/")
        await page.fill("#topic", TOPIC)
        await page.click("#startBtn")
        await page.wait_for_selector(".status-bar", timeout=10000)
        await page.screenshot(path=str(SCREENSHOT_DIR / "03_submit_progress.png"))

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_04_adapter_results(self, browser_page):
        page, errors = browser_page
        await page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/web/")
        await page.fill("#topic", TOPIC)
        await page.click("#startBtn")
        try:
            await page.wait_for_selector(".adapter-row", timeout=60000)
            await page.screenshot(path=str(SCREENSHOT_DIR / "04_adapter_results.png"))
            rows = await page.query_selector_all(".adapter-row")
            assert len(rows) >= 1
        except Exception:
            await page.screenshot(path=str(SCREENSHOT_DIR / "04_adapter_results.png"))
            pytest.skip("adapter_result not received in time")

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_05_filter_result(self, browser_page):
        page, errors = browser_page
        await page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/web/")

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror search content tools. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 100). Use to limit output for broad patterns. When the cap is hit the result is paginated; pass 'offset' set to the previous offset+maxResults to continue.

offset.description: Optional: Number of matching file paths to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text files. For images, PDFs, videos, audio or other multimedia files, this tool cannot present their contents to the model — use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool instead.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once. Note: search_file_content reports matches as 1-based `Lxx` line numbers; to jump from a hit at `L42` here, subtract 1 — pass `offset: 41` (i.e. `offset = lineNumber - 1`).

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. The search backend is ripgrep, so regex patterns use ripgrep/Rust regex syntax, not JavaScript RegExp syntax. If you need to search literal text that contains regex metacharacters (for example $, {, [, (, or \), set fixed_strings: true or -F: true instead of escaping by hand. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. By default, returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers; set output_mode to "files_with_matches" for just file paths or "count" for per-file match totals. Each matched line is truncated at 500 characters with a "[line truncated from N chars]" suffix to keep minified or base64 content from blowing up context.

pattern.description: The regular expression pattern to search for within file contents. This is interpreted by ripgrep/Rust regex, not JavaScript RegExp; avoid JS-only features such as lookbehind and backreferences. Regex syntax errors are returned by ripgrep. For literal text, especially strings containing regex metacharacters like $, {, [, (, or \, prefer fixed_strings: true or -F: true. When passing JSON args, escape only for JSON once: to match literal ${, use pattern "\\$\\{" (the regex engine sees \$\{), not "\\\\$\\\\{".

path.description: Optional: A path relative to the workspace root, or an absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

glob.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Single string only — combine multiple patterns via whitespace/comma separation (e.g. '*.js *.ts', '*.js,*.ts') or brace expansion ('*.{ts,tsx}'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

output_mode.description: Optional: Output format. 'content' (default) returns matching lines with line numbers and optional context; 'files_with_matches' returns only the list of matching file paths; 'count' returns per-file match counts.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 250, max: 1000). Values above 1000 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches. Pass 0 for unlimited.

head_limit.description: Optional: Claude Code aligned alias for 'maxResults'. When both are provided, head_limit wins. Pass 0 for unlimited (use sparingly — large result sets waste context).

offset.description: Optional: Number of matches to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

-A.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -A.

-B.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -B.

-C.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -C.

context.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for -C.

-n.description: Optional: Whether to include line numbers in 'content' output. Defaults to true; set to false to omit them.

-i.description: Optional: Case-insensitive matching. Defaults to false (case-sensitive), matching ripgrep behavior.

fixed_strings.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for -F.

-F.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for fixed_strings.

type.description: Optional: ripgrep file type filter (rg --type). Common types: js, py, rust, go, java, etc. Accepts any alias from `rg --type-list`; the value is forwarded verbatim to ripgrep.

multiline.description: Optional: Enable multiline matching where patterns may span newlines (passes -U and --multiline-dotall to ripgrep). Only meaningful for 'content' output_mode. ^/$ anchors keep line boundaries behavior for portability across search backends.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror ripgrep's default hidden-skip behaviour. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly (passes --hidden to ripgrep).

functionDeclarations.name: complete_task
functionDeclarations.description:
Call this tool to submit your final result and complete the task. This is the ONLY way to finish.

This tool must be called ALONE in its turn. Do not call any other tools in the same turn. If you still need another tool, do not call this yet.

Your result MUST be an informative handoff for the caller (main agent), not a one-line summary.

Requirements for result:
- Use 2+ short paragraphs or a short markdown sectioned report
- Include what you did / found and the concrete evidence that supports it
- Include file paths, errors, command outcomes, or other artifacts when relevant
- Do NOT return only a title or a single-line summary
- Keep it concise, but detailed enough that the main agent can act without reopening your full trace
